articles.title: Bond Investors Flee as Inflation Worry Sends Yields to 2026 High
articles.summary: Investors are fleeing government bonds after back-to-back US inflation reports this week showed mounting price pressures, sending benchmark interest rates to the highest levels in nearly a year.

articles.title: Intertek Inclined to Recommend EQT’s Final £9.2B Offer
articles.summary: Bloomberg's Crystal Tse joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Intertek Group is leaning toward recommending a fourth and final takeover offer from private equity firm EQT AB. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Oil Holds Steady on Mideast Impasse and Shrinking Inventories
articles.summary: Oil largely held its 8% surge over the past three sessions as Middle East tensions simmer and global stockpiles shrink at a record pace.

articles.title: Hormuz Oil Flows Fell Nearly 30% Last Quarter, EIA Says

articles.title: Ford Shares Soar After Morgan Stanley’s Bullish Call on Energy
articles.summary: Ford Motor Co. shares surged Wednesday after a bullish call from Morgan Stanley on the automaker drew investor attention to the company’s energy storage business.

articles.title: Treasury Buyers Get 5% Long-Bond Rate for First Time Since 2007
articles.summary: Investors snagged 5% yields on 30-year Treasuries for the first time since 2007, as surging energy prices push inflation — and expectations for more of it — higher.

articles.title: Geothermal Energy Firm Fervo Rises 33% After $1.89 Billion IPO
articles.summary: Fervo Energy Co. shares opened 33% above their IPO price after the geothermal energy developer raised $1.89 billion in an upsized US initial public offering.

articles.title: NYC College Merger Brings $203 Million Windfall for Northeastern
articles.summary: Northeastern University’s takeover of New York City’s Marymount Manhattan College provided a $202.7 million windfall, bolstered by its new ownership of prime Manhattan real estate.

articles.title: Stocks Get Tech Boost Despite Pickup in Inflation: Markets Wrap
